title: nie-bot: script hook safety tests
description:
What: Unit test suite verifying shlex safety boundary and hook execution behavior.

Algorithm (tests in bot/src/scripting.rs #[cfg(test)]):
1. test_hook_echo: run_hook("echo hello", &[]) → exit 0, stdout contains "hello"
   Oracle: spawn actual echo process; expected output "hello\n" known independently
2. test_hook_unmatched_quote: run_hook("echo 'bad", &[]) → Err(BotError::Config(_)) — unmatched quote rejected before exec
3. test_hook_no_sh_metachar: run_hook("echo foo;cat /etc/passwd", &[]) → exit 0, stdout contains literal "foo;cat" (not executed as pipeline)
   This test PROVES shell injection is blocked: if sh -c were used, /etc/passwd would appear in stdout
4. test_hook_env_var_passed: run_hook("env", &[("NIE_FROM".into(), "testpub".into())]) → stdout contains NIE_FROM=testpub
5. test_hook_timeout: run_hook("sleep 60", &[]) with 1s timeout → Err(BotError::ScriptTimeout{..})
   (requires making timeout duration configurable or using a test-only short timeout)

Defensive: test 3 is a SECURITY test — assert stdout does NOT contain any line from /etc/passwd.

Agent team: T (tester — expected values from shell behavior, not from scripting.rs code).
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-bot bot::scripting passes; test 3 (injection prevention) present and passing; all 5 tests pass
